title: Vegetable Storage
content:
STORAGE

Do not store with apples (ethylene gas makes carrots bitter) or onions (hastens potato spoilage). Cut leaves from root vegetables immediately (sap continues to leaf at expense of root). Salad greens, celery, beans hold better in plastic bags in vegetable keeper. Potatoes in dark place to prevent greening and toxic quality development.

Temperatures:
- Lettuce, celery, beans, endive: 35°F moderate humidity
- Cabbage family, most vegetables: 35-40°F
- Potatoes, tomatoes: around 40°F
- Onions, garlic, dried peas/beans: 35°F dry conditions
- To ripen sweet potatoes and tomatoes: 60-70°F warm dry air
